# Questions in Livestream 70

Live Stream #70: For those in the Know

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3X9HQiOdJOk?t=373" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">6:13</a> | What was the family structure in ancient Egypt like, and was it similar to the modern nuclear family? | He said it was somewhat similar, but usually broader, with servants or slaves and sometimes elderly parents treated as part of the household. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3X9HQiOdJOk?t=567" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">9:27</a> | What are declensions, and did Egyptian have them? | Yes. He said Egyptian nouns and related forms do show declensional patterns, though far less elaborately than languages like Greek. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3X9HQiOdJOk?t=674" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">11:14</a> | What did `a land flowing with milk and honey` mean to early Israelites, and was it an Egyptian idiom? | He said the phrase does appear in Egyptian material, but in the Bible it points to pastoral abundance, especially goat's milk and wild honey, rather than settled agricultural security. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3X9HQiOdJOk?t=870" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:30</a> | Which paranormal podcasts does he listen to or recommend? | He recommended shows such as Weird Darkness and Unexplained Encounters, mainly as broad paranormal or mystery listening rather than as academic sources. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3X9HQiOdJOk?t=1142" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">19:02</a> | Can he recommend a good history of the mystery schools? | Not really. He said the field is too broad and fractured, and he does not know a single book that covers it well. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3X9HQiOdJOk?t=1422" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">23:42</a> | Does the Pelasgian creation myth with Eurynome bruising Ophion's head create a problem for Genesis 3:15? | No. He said that version is a modern reconstruction by Robert Graves rather than an authentic ancient myth parallel. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3X9HQiOdJOk?t=1909" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">31:49</a> | Is prophetic literature meant to work through multiple different contextual fulfillments across generations? | He said prophetic literature is not fatalism and is often recontextualized by later generations, so differing readings may reflect new contexts rather than simple error. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3X9HQiOdJOk?t=2349" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">39:09</a> | Why was the New Testament written in Greek rather than Hebrew or Aramaic? | He said Greek was the common language of the Roman world and diaspora Jewish communities, making it the practical language for reaching the intended audience. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3X9HQiOdJOk?t=2842" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">47:22</a> | What alternatives to the documentary hypothesis exist for Pentateuch composition? | He mentioned supplementary and redactional models as the main competitors, while arguing that each may contain some truth without adequately displacing a fundamentally Mosaic core. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3X9HQiOdJOk?t=3350" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">55:50</a> | What does he make of the recently found rare Ramesses II era burial in Israel? | He said it is intriguing but still too unpublished to assess closely; for now it looks Egyptian-period and probably Canaanite rather than Israelite in character. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3X9HQiOdJOk?t=3484" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">58:04</a> | In 1 Corinthians 6:15-16, does becoming one flesh apply to sex in general, and what about rape? | He said it probably applies to normal voluntary heterosexual intercourse, but he was uncertain whether involuntary assault fits the one-flesh union because the issue is debated. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3X9HQiOdJOk?t=3610" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:00:10</a> | What happens if someone blasphemes the Holy Spirit? | He treated it as a very specific sin tied to accusing Jesus directly of demonic possession, making it largely a sin of Christ's historical setting rather than a common modern occurrence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3X9HQiOdJOk?t=3901" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:05:01</a> | What does he think about the Shroud of Turin? | He was doubtful, citing weak provenance and the tension between the one-piece shroud and biblical passages that seem to describe separate body and face cloths. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3X9HQiOdJOk?t=4543" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:15:43</a> | Are there examples of pre-modern knowledge that seem surprisingly close to modern science? | He said some ancient thinkers made impressive observations, but most alleged examples are controversial because the ancient and modern ideas often are not really the same thing. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3X9HQiOdJOk?t=4916" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:21:56</a> | What about the ancient Greek computer, the Antikythera mechanism? | He said it was not a computer in the modern sense, but a sophisticated geared mechanism probably used to model astronomical cycles and ritual calendars. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3X9HQiOdJOk?t=5166" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:26:06</a> | Is Egypt's current population and inhabited land area sustainable, or will Egypt collapse? | He did not expect imminent collapse, citing agricultural expansion, better food supply, and possible future population decline, while noting uncertainty and political risk. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3X9HQiOdJOk?t=5752" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:35:52</a> | In Daniel 9:27, did he say the Hebrew reflects the wave sheaf offering? | He said he did not think he had said that; the text refers more generally to sacrifice and grain offering, requiring a temple or sacred space. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3X9HQiOdJOk?t=6102" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:41:42</a> | Has he heard of the Samson seal from Beth Shemesh, and what does he make of it? | He said it is provocative evidence for a Samson-like motif because it shows a man fighting a lion at Beth Shemesh, but without writing it is not decisive proof of Samson. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3X9HQiOdJOk?t=6340" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:45:40</a> | Does Gnosticism depend on Kabbalah? | No. He said Gnosticism long predates Kabbalah, though medieval Kabbalah may have absorbed some Gnostic or Hermetic influence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3X9HQiOdJOk?t=6499" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:48:19</a> | Is there significance to the lack of a definite article before the numbered days in Genesis 1? | He did not think the missing article proves much either way; at most it shows the days are being sequentially framed, not whether they must be literal 24-hour periods. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3X9HQiOdJOk?t=6770" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:52:50</a> | If the Merneptah Stele says Jezreel rather than Israel, what is the next historical reference to Israel, and how late could the Exodus be? | He rejected the Jezreel reading, said the next clear reference is probably the Mesha Stele, and placed the latest possible Exodus before the Merneptah Stele with a preference nearer 1265 BC. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3X9HQiOdJOk?t=7489" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:04:49</a> | Does he think Isaiah 40-66 was written by Isaiah or by later disciples? | He favored Isaiah himself, arguing that the book's large-scale reflective structure looks intentional even if the material may have been written and arranged over time. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3X9HQiOdJOk?t=7842" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:10:42</a> | Would he take down his SG and play guitar for a larger Super Chat? | No. He said he lives in an apartment, composes more than he performs, and would not play loudly on stream. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3X9HQiOdJOk?t=8304" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:18:24</a> | What is going on with Esau being so noticeably red and hairy from birth in Genesis 25:25? | He said the description foreshadows Esau's hyper-masculine, physical, hunter-oriented character in contrast with Jacob's cleverness and spiritual inheritance theme. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3X9HQiOdJOk?t=8808" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:26:48</a> | What Old Testament scene would he most like to witness in person? | He chose Abraham's encounter with the Egyptian king, wanting to see the court setting, the ruler involved, and the details the text leaves unstated. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3X9HQiOdJOk?t=8962" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:29:22</a> | How would a reader or listener notice a chiasmus spanning ten chapters of Genesis, and how would missing it affect interpretation? | He said readers are more likely than listeners to notice large-scale structures, but repeated chiastic patterns suggest intentional literary design that can shape interpretation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3X9HQiOdJOk?t=9668" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:41:08</a> | When and why would the vav in Kush in Genesis 2 have been added if it was originally Kish? | He said the vav likely functions as a mater added during Hebrew standardization, probably around the eleventh to ninth centuries BC, and may not have been original. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3X9HQiOdJOk?t=10043" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:47:23</a> | What does he think of Peter Feinman's idea that the Exodus involved Hyksos Levites leaving Egypt under Ramesses II? | He rejected the Levite-Hyksos version, arguing that Hyksos refers to foreign rulers, the proposed date is too early, the settlement evidence needs more than Levites, and the theory depends too heavily on the documentary hypothesis. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3X9HQiOdJOk?t=10914" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:01:54</a> | If Revelation 19 is Christ's second coming and the white-throne judgment follows a thousand years later, does that imply a third coming? | He said no; he treated the thousand years as an idiomatic period and the white-throne judgment as humanity being brought before God rather than Christ returning a third time. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/3X9HQiOdJOk?t=11379" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:09:39</a> | Was music involved in courting Mrs. Falk? | No. He said their shared connection was more through reading and science fiction than through music. |
